original_hypothesis.title: TwoWay LockedStatus Defined but Not Implemented — Missing Reverse Exchange Functionality
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The LockedStatus enum defines three states: Locked, OneWay, and TwoWay. However, the codebase treats OneWay and TwoWay identically — both simply allow TGT→TITN deposits in onTokenTransfer. No reverse exchange (TITN→TGT) function exists. If TwoWay was intended to enable bidirectional exchange, this is missing implementation. Users or governance expecting TwoWay mode to allow returning TGT have no recourse.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: onTokenTransfer checks `lockedStatus == LockedStatus.Locked` — it reverts only if Locked. Both OneWay and TwoWay pass the check identically. No code path exists for a TITN→TGT reverse swap, suggesting either incomplete implementation or misleading enum design.

proof_of_signal.pattern: if (lockedStatus == LockedStatus.Locked || launchTime == 0) revert; — no distinction between OneWay and TwoWay

preconditions: Owner sets lockedStatus to TwoWay expecting bidirectional exchange
Users expect ability to reverse their TGT deposit

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: TwoWay enum value defined but functionally identical to OneWay in all code paths
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Missing reverse exchange function that TwoWay should enable
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No TITN→TGT swap function; no differentiation of OneWay vs TwoWay behavior

analysis_questions: Was TwoWay intended to enable reverse exchanges?
Is there documentation describing TwoWay behavior?
Is this dead code or a missing feature?
validation_requirements: Verify no new functionality is enabled compared to OneWay
Search for any code that checks for TwoWay specifically

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Governance/owner sets TwoWay expecting users can return TITN for TGT. Users deposit TGT, then discover they cannot reverse the exchange. If TGT price rises relative to TITN, users are stuck with an unfavorable one-way conversion they expected to be reversible.
prerequisites: Expectation that TwoWay enables reverse exchange

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Users unable to reverse unfavorable exchanges
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: Users who deposited TGT expecting TwoWay reversibility
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Only owner can withdraw TGT and manually return — no automated path

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
